It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to relieve fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chusua taiwanensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
